glossary.def: A neural network trained to reconstruct its own input through a constricted intermediate code. An encoder compresses the input to a low-dimensional code and a decoder rebuilds it; because the code is narrower than the input, the network must learn the data's underlying structure rather than copy it. Training needs no labels (self-supervised).

The narrow intermediate layer of an autoencoder whose width k is smaller than the input dimension d. It forces the network to discard everything except the few axes of greatest variation, so the code becomes a compressed coordinate system for the data manifold.

The space of all codes an encoder can produce. Distances in a good latent space track perceptual or semantic similarity far better than raw input distance, enabling interpolation, clustering, retrieval, and anomaly detection by reconstruction error.

An autoencoder fed a corrupted input and trained to output the clean original. Copying is impossible, so the network must learn the shape of the data and project corrupted points back onto the manifold; this implicitly estimates the score (the gradient of the log-density), the seed idea behind diffusion models.

A probabilistic autoencoder whose encoder outputs a distribution (mean and variance) rather than a point, with a loss that regularizes that distribution toward a standard-normal prior. The result is a smooth, gap-free latent space you can sample from, turning the autoencoder into a generative model.

The VAE's training objective: a lower bound on the data log-likelihood equal to the expected reconstruction term minus the KL divergence from the approximate posterior to the prior. Maximizing the ELBO is equivalent to minimizing reconstruction error plus KL-to-prior.

Writing a latent sample as z = mu + sigma * eps with eps drawn from a fixed standard normal, so the only randomness sits in eps and gradients flow through mu and sigma. It makes the stochastic VAE trainable end-to-end by ordinary backpropagation.

A VAE failure mode in which a powerful decoder learns to ignore the latent code; the KL term then drives the approximate posterior all the way to the prior, so z carries no information. KL annealing, free-bits, and weaker decoders are the usual remedies.

refs.cite: Hinton, G. E. & Salakhutdinov, R. R. (2006). Reducing the Dimensionality of Data with Neural Networks. Science 313(5786).
Kingma, D. P. & Welling, M. (2013). Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes. ICLR 2014.
Vincent, P., Larochelle, H., Bengio, Y. & Manzagol, P.-A. (2008). Extracting and Composing Robust Features with Denoising Autoencoders. ICML 2008.
Baldi, P. & Hornik, K. (1989). Neural Networks and Principal Component Analysis: Learning from Examples without Local Minima. Neural Networks 2(1).
Higgins, I. et al. (2017). beta-VAE: Learning Basic Visual Concepts with a Constrained Variational Framework. ICLR 2017.
van den Oord, A., Vinyals, O. & Kavukcuoglu, K. (2017). Neural Discrete Representation Learning (VQ-VAE). NeurIPS 2017.
Rombach, R., Blattmann, A., Lorenz, D., Esser, P. & Ommer, B. (2022). High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models. CVPR 2022.
He, K., Chen, X., Xie, S., Li, Y., Dollar, P. & Girshick, R. (2022). Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners. CVPR 2022.
